$schema: Design-brief eval set for the harness-studio skill. Each brief is built by a builder agent following the skill, then graded deterministically by evals/grade.mjs. 'approvedDirection' lets the build-quality arm skip the interactive brainstorm (brainstorm-first is graded separately, cold-open). split: train = diagnose+fix on these; heldout = stop-condition only, never fit to them.
_serious_note: These gate A5 (any one = fail). 'numbered-section-markers' was REMOVED from gating: in data-dense APP screens it false-positives on numeric data (metric values like 0.02/0.08, Tailwind opacity utilities like bg-white/[.02]) — it's a marketing-page tell, unreliable for app UIs. It still appears in the full findings (designReview.byId) for visibility, just non-gating.

briefs.title: Coffee subscription checkout
briefs.prompt: Design the checkout flow for a specialty coffee subscription. The customer reviews their box, edits quantity, enters shipping, picks a plan cadence, and confirms. It should feel like a premium independent roaster, not a generic Shopify theme.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 3-screen web checkout for 'Meridian Roasters': (1) Review box — line items with quantity steppers and an order summary; (2) Shipping & cadence — address form + weekly/biweekly/monthly cadence choice; (3) Confirm — order placed with summary. Premium editorial roaster feel. Shared header (logo + cart badge) and footer across all screens.

briefs.title: Observability SaaS dashboard
briefs.prompt: Design the main dashboard for an observability/monitoring SaaS (think uptime, latency, error rate across services). A logged-in engineer lands here, scans health, and drills into one service. Data-dense but calm and legible.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 2-3 screen desktop-web dashboard for 'Pulse': (1) Overview — service health cards, latency/error sparml charts, recent incidents; (2) Service detail — one service's metrics + recent deploys; optional (3) Incidents list. Shared left nav + top bar. Technical, calm, legible — not a marketing page.

briefs.title: Meditation app onboarding
briefs.prompt: Design the first-run onboarding for a meditation app: a welcome splash, a couple of personalization steps (goal, experience level), and a finish screen that lands them in their first session. It's the user's very first impression.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 4-screen iOS onboarding for 'Still': (1) Splash/welcome (full-bleed, no chrome); (2) Pick your goal (choice list); (3) Experience level (choice list with a progress indicator); (4) You're set — start first session. Calm, premium, full-bleed where it counts. Shared progress component on steps 2-3.

briefs.title: Longform journalism home + article
briefs.prompt: Design a longform journalism publication: a home page that frames the lead story and a few features, plus an article reading view. Typography is the product. Should feel like a serious, modern magazine.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 2-screen web publication for 'The Meridian Review': (1) Home — a strong lead story, a small grid of features, a section rail; (2) Article — a single longform piece with confident reading typography. Shared masthead + footer. Type-forward, editorial, restrained.

briefs.title: Clinic admin — patients
briefs.prompt: Design the patients section of a small clinic's admin tool: a list/table of patients with status, and an add/edit patient form. Form-heavy, dense, but humane — staff use it all day.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 2-3 screen desktop-web admin for 'Carehub': (1) Patients — searchable table with status badges and a primary 'Add patient' action; (2) Patient form — add/edit with grouped fields; optional (3) Patient detail. Shared left nav + header. Dense, legible, calm — a real internal tool.

briefs.title: Restaurant reservation
briefs.prompt: Design a restaurant reservation flow on mobile: browse availability, pick a date/time and party size, and confirm the booking. A polished, modern dining brand.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 3-screen iOS reservation flow for 'Cardoon': (1) Restaurant — hero, info, a 'Reserve' CTA; (2) Pick date/time + party size (selectable chips that set store); (3) Confirm booking. Shared header. Polished modern dining brand, full-bleed hero.

briefs.title: Sales CRM pipeline
briefs.prompt: Design the deal pipeline view of a sales CRM: deals across stages, a way to open one deal, and the deal detail. For a salesperson working their book daily.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 2-3 screen desktop-web CRM for 'Closer': (1) Pipeline — columns by stage with deal cards (value, company, owner); (2) Deal detail — one deal's timeline, contact, next step. Shared left nav + top bar. Confident, dense, calm SaaS.

briefs.title: Workout tracker
briefs.prompt: Design a workout tracker on Android: today's plan, an active workout screen where you log sets/reps, and a simple progress view. Energetic but not loud.
briefs.approvedDirection: A 3-screen Android tracker for 'Forge': (1) Today — the day's planned workout, start CTA; (2) Active workout — exercises with set/rep counters (data-inc/dec on a live store); (3) Progress — weekly summary. Shared bottom tab bar. Energetic, clean, full-bleed where it helps.
